{"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 2.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:L/VI:N/VA:N/SC:L/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-200", "name": "Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-681", "name": "Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types"}], "description": "### Impact

Wasmtime's Winch compiler contains a bug where a 64-bit table, part of the memory64 proposal of WebAssembly, incorrectly translated the `table.size` instruction. This bug could lead to disclosing data on the host's stack to WebAssembly guests. The host's stack can possibly contain sensitive data related to other host-originating operations which is not intended to be disclosed to guests.

This bug specifically arose from a mistake where the return value of `table.size` was statically typed as a 32-bit integer, as opposed to consulting the table's index type to see how large the returned register could be. When combined with details about Wnich's ABI, such as multi-value returns, this can be combined to read stack data from the host, within a guest. This information disclosure should not be possible in WebAssembly, violates spec semantics, and is a vulnerability in Wasmtime.

### Patches

Wasmtime 36.0.7, 42.0.2, and 43.0.1 have been issued to fix this bug. Users are recommended to update to these patched versions of Wasmtime.

### Workarounds

Users of Cranelift are not affected by this issue, but users of Winch have no workarounds other than disabling the `Config::wasm_memory64` proposal.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00324, "percentile": 0.23964}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-09T20:23:02Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-34945"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-09T19:16:24Z", "published_at": "2026-04-09T20:23:02Z", "references": ["https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wasmtime/security/advisories/GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-34945", "https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0086.html", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/bytecodealliance/wasmtime/security-advisories/GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wasmtime", "summary": "Wasmtime has host data leakage with 64-bit tables and Winch", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T21:03:34Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-m9w2-8782-2946", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "36.0.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "wasmtime"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 25.0.0, < 36.0.7"}, {"first_patched_version": "42.0.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "wasmtime"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 37.0.0, < 42.0.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "43.0.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "wasmtime"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "= 43.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Wasmtime has host data leakage with 64-bit tables and Winch
